verified_claims.report_excerpt: Nvidia has delivered a remarkable financial performance over the past three years, with revenue surging from $26.9 billion in FY2023 to $60.9 billion in FY2024, representing a 125.9% year-over-year increase.
verified_claims.claim_quote: revenue surging from $26.9 billion in FY2023 to $60.9 billion in FY2024
verified_claims.normalized_claim: NVDA revenue was approximately $26.9B in FY2023 and $60.9B in FY2024.
verified_claims.extraction_note: Split from the same sentence; this task verifies the two revenue values, not the growth calculation.

verification_source.document_title: NVIDIA Corporation Form 10-K for fiscal year ended January 28, 2024

verified_claims.evidence_excerpt: Revenue table reports total revenue of $60,922 million for Jan 28, 2024 and $26,974 million for Jan 29, 2023.
verified_claims.calculation_trace: 60,922 million = $60.922B, displayed as $60.9B. 26,974 million = $26.974B; the report value $26.9B is truncation-compatible at one decimal place, not ordinary rounding. Under NUM_ABS_001 alone, the FY2023 relative difference is about 0.274%, above the 0.1% relative tolerance; display-precision handling is therefore required.
verified_claims.review_note: Supported with presentation caveat. FY2024 is one-decimal compatible; FY2023 is truncation-compatible, not ordinary rounding. Report-level traceability remains weak.
verified_claims.presentation_caveat: B01 remains eligible L2 but is excluded from strict supported count because FY2023 is truncation-compatible rather than ordinary rounding-compatible.

verified_claims.report_excerpt: Nvidia has delivered a remarkable financial performance over the past three years, with revenue surging from $26.9 billion in FY2023 to $60.9 billion in FY2024, representing a 125.9% year-over-year increase.
verified_claims.normalized_claim: NVDA FY2024 revenue growth from FY2023 revenue was 125.9%.
verified_claims.extraction_note: Derived calculation separated from the two reported revenue values.

verification_source.document_title: NVIDIA Corporation Form 10-K for fiscal year ended January 28, 2024

verified_claims.evidence_excerpt: Revenue table reports $60,922 million for FY2024 and $26,974 million for FY2023, with total revenue change of 126%.
verified_claims.calculation_trace: (60,922 - 26,974) / 26,974 = 1.25862 = 125.9% when rounded to one decimal place.
verified_claims.review_note: Supported using exact source values; report's rounded revenue display would not independently reproduce 125.9%.

verified_claims.report_excerpt: The company's EBITDA has also accelerated, reaching $35.6 billion in FY2024 and expected to climb to $86.1 billion in FY2025 and $144.6 billion in FY2026.
verified_claims.claim_quote: EBITDA has also accelerated, reaching $35.6 billion in FY2024
verified_claims.extraction_note: Historical EBITDA separated from forward EBITDA estimates.

verification_source.document_title: NVIDIA Corporation Form 10-K for fiscal year ended January 28, 2024

verified_claims.evidence_excerpt: Mapped primary source reports GAAP operating income and other statement items, but does not directly report EBITDA as a GAAP line item.
verified_claims.review_note: Do not promote to L2 without a disclosed EBITDA definition and reproducible inputs. FinRobot/FMP table is not final verification ground truth.

verified_claims.report_excerpt: Margins have expanded significantly, with EBITDA margin improving from 22.2% in FY2023 to 58.4% in FY2024 and projected to reach over 70% by FY2027.
verified_claims.claim_quote: EBITDA margin improving from 22.2% in FY2023 to 58.4% in FY2024
verified_claims.normalized_claim: NVDA EBITDA margin was 22.2% in FY2023 and 58.4% in FY2024.
verified_claims.extraction_note: Historical margin claim separated from FY2027 projection.

verification_source.document_title: NVIDIA Corporation Form 10-K for fiscal year ended January 28, 2024

verified_claims.evidence_excerpt: Mapped primary source reports gross margin, SG&A percentage, operating income, and net income; EBITDA margin is not directly reported.
verified_claims.review_note: EBITDA margin depends on EBITDA definition. Without a reproducible EBITDA definition, this is not a deterministic L2 task.

verified_claims.report_excerpt: Operational leverage is evident, with SG&A as a percentage of revenue declining from 9% in FY2023 to just 2.1% in FY2026, supporting substantial contribution margin improvement.
verified_claims.claim_quote: SG&A as a percentage of revenue declining from 9% in FY2023 to just 2.1% in FY2026
verified_claims.normalized_claim: NVDA SG&A margin was approximately 9% in FY2023 and 2.1% in FY2026.
verified_claims.extraction_note: Numeric SG&A margin claim separated from 'operational leverage is evident' inference.

verification_source.document_title: NVIDIA Corporation Form 10-K for fiscal year ended January 25, 2026

verified_claims.evidence_excerpt: FY2026 10-K consolidated statement reports revenue of $215,938 million and sales, general and administrative expense of $4,579 million for Jan 25, 2026.
verified_claims.calculation_trace: 4,579 / 215,938 = 0.02121 = 2.1%. FY2023 9% is supported within the predefined 0.1 percentage-point tolerance by FY2024 10-K common-size statement showing sales, general and administrative expense at 9.1% for FY2023.
verified_claims.review_note: Supported after evaluator reconstructed the source path. Numeric claim supported does not mean the operational leverage inference was scored. Report does not identify the 2026 10-K or calculation basis.

verified_claims.report_excerpt: This explosive growth is projected to continue, with consensus estimates pointing to $130.5 billion in revenue for FY2025 and $215.9 billion for FY2026—a 68.3% CAGR through FY2027.
verified_claims.normalized_claim: The report states a 68.3% revenue CAGR through FY2027.
verified_claims.extraction_note: CAGR endpoint is ambiguous because the sentence quotes FY2025 and FY2026 values but says through FY2027.
verified_claims.evidence_excerpt: No source-mapped CAGR endpoint has been recorded. The report does not identify whether the CAGR runs from FY2023, FY2024, FY2025, or another base period through FY2027.
verified_claims.review_note: Backlog because CAGR period and endpoint are not sufficiently specified for deterministic scoring. Re-entry requires explicit start period, end period, and source values.

verified_claims.report_excerpt: Compared to peers, Nvidia's EBITDA outpaces AMD by a wide margin, and its EV/EBITDA valuation, while high (43.5x in FY2024), is trending lower (31.4x in FY2026) as earnings scale.
verified_claims.claim_quote: EV/EBITDA valuation, while high (43.5x in FY2024), is trending lower (31.4x in FY2026)
verified_claims.normalized_claim: NVDA EV/EBITDA is 43.5x in FY2024 and 31.4x in FY2026.
verified_claims.extraction_note: Valuation multiple lacks dated market-value and EBITDA basis in the report; not L1/L2 eligible without reproducible source.
verified_claims.evidence_excerpt: No dated market value, net debt/enterprise value basis, or EBITDA basis has been mapped for this claim.
verified_claims.review_note: Backlog because EV/EBITDA requires dated market value, net debt or EV basis, and EBITDA definition. These inputs are not identified by the report.

verified_claims.report_excerpt: While the stock trades at a premium (PE ratio of 37.7x FY2026E), this reflects Nvidia's dominant competitive position and robust long-term growth prospects.
verified_claims.normalized_claim: The report states NVDA FY2026E PE ratio is 37.7x.
verified_claims.extraction_note: Forward PE requires dated price and EPS estimate basis.
verified_claims.evidence_excerpt: No dated price source or FY2026E EPS estimate source has been mapped for this claim.
verified_claims.review_note: Backlog because forward PE requires a dated share price and EPS estimate source. These inputs are not identified by the report.
